---
title: "Simple-mode defaults"
description: "What gets auto-configured when you pick a pack + profile in simple mode."
---

Simple mode (`--simple`, or the default when you pass `-y`) asks you for only two things: **pack** and **profile**. Everything else is derived from smart defaults. This page lists every defaulted value so there are no surprises.

## Instance size

| Profile | Instance |
|---|---|
| `builder` | `t4g.xlarge` (4 vCPU, 16 GiB) |
| `account_assistant` | `t4g.medium` (2 vCPU, 4 GiB) |
| `personal_assistant` | `t4g.medium` |

All instances are arm64 Graviton by default.

## Security services

| Profile | Security Hub | GuardDuty | Inspector | Access Analyzer | Config recorder |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `builder` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `account_assistant` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `personal_assistant` | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

See [Security services](/reference/security-services) for what each one does.

## Region

Default: `us-east-1`. Override with `AWS_REGION` / `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION` env var, or by picking advanced mode.

## Volumes

| Pack | Root volume | Data volume |
|---|---|---|
| openclaw | 40 GB | 80 GB |
| claude-code | 40 GB | 0 GB |
| codex-cli | 40 GB | 0 GB |
| kiro-cli | 40 GB | 0 GB |
| nemoclaw | 40 GB | 80 GB |
| hermes | 40 GB | 0 GB |
| pi | 40 GB | 0 GB |
| ironclaw | 40 GB | 0 GB |

## VPC

Simple mode creates a **new VPC** by default. Advanced mode prompts for VPC reuse if any tagged `loki:managed=true` VPC exists in the region.

| Setting | Default |
|---|---|
| VPC CIDR | `10.0.0.0/16` |
| Public subnet | `10.0.1.0/24` |
| Internet gateway | Yes (public subnet) |
| NAT gateway | No (instance has public IP) |

## IAM

The EC2 instance gets an instance profile with the profile-appropriate policy:

| Profile | Managed policy |
|---|---|
| `builder` | `arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess` |
| `account_assistant` | `arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess` + targeted tag/log writes |
| `personal_assistant` | Inline policy: Bedrock `InvokeModel` only |

All profiles also get `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` so you can SSM in.

## SSH

Default: **disabled**. `SSHAllowedCidr=127.0.0.1/32` = no one can reach SSH. Use SSM Session Manager.

Override in advanced mode by setting a real CIDR (e.g. `1.2.3.4/32` for your current IP).

## Model defaults

Each pack has its own default model. The installer threads the right default per pack — you never have to think about it:

| Pack | Default model |
|---|---|
| openclaw | `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1` |
| claude-code | `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` (main) + `us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0` (haiku) |
| codex-cli | `gpt-5.4` |
| kiro-cli | — (Kiro cloud picks its own) |
| nemoclaw | `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` (via bedrockify) |
| hermes | `anthropic/claude-opus-4.6` (OpenAI-style via bedrockify) |
| pi | `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6-v1` (via bedrockify) |
| ironclaw | `anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` (via bedrockify) |

## Environment naming

Simple mode auto-generates an environment name: `<pack>-<sequence>-<timestamp-suffix>`.

Example: `openclaw-1-4521`.

The sequence number counts existing `loki:managed=true` VPCs in the region so you don't collide.

## Loki watermark tag

Every resource created by Lowkey is tagged `loki:managed=true` + `loki:watermark=<env-name>`. This makes it easy to find and clean up everything the installer created.

```bash
aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources \
  --tag-filters Key=loki:managed,Values=true
```

## What simple mode does NOT auto-decide

You still provide:

- **Pack** (required)
- **Profile** (defaults to `builder` if `-y` given with no `--profile`)
- **Deploy method** (defaults to CloudFormation CLI)

And anything you override explicitly via flags wins over the default.
